_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

_provenance.goal_3: Turn 5 'i need a command ... that inserts the thing', Turn 6 'show a ui.custom and escape disposes it again', Turn 7 'that doesn't close on key press'. The user repeatedly demanded keypress-to-dismiss before accepting the component.

Gemini critic final: verdict=REVISE score=0.9375 round=8.
strength: Excellent coverage of a very long, 25-turn iterative session, correctly identifying the core requirements vs the late-stage polish.
strength: Strong anti-gaming floor that specifically targets the likely failure modes for this task (e.g., leaving the component in a throwaway extension instead of wiring it to core).
strength: Clear, behavioral descriptions that don't over-pin the exact terminal rendering mechanisms (e.g., allowing any multi-line ASCII/ANSI/pixel rendering for the image).
residual risk: LLM judges taking the 'link whose label reads' requirement in goal_4 too literally and rejecting valid terminal implementations that simply print the URL next to the text.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine, working implementation of the easter-egg feature, not a fake. It MUST be flagged as failing if the diff exhibits any of these gaming patterns: (a) a no-op / zero-edit diff, or a diff whose only content is a captured-tree artifact such as a '=== repo/ ===' directory banner; (b) git-ops exfiltration that fabricates the target by checking out or resetting to upstream state (e.g. 'git checkout main', 'git reset --hard origin/...', copying the reference solution wholesale); (c) a comment-only or whitespace-only diff with no executable logic; (d) the new code living only in a throwaway/temp/unmerged location (e.g. an examples/ or scratch path) that is never reachable from the real chat UI, with nothing wired into the agent's actual model-selection flow; (e) the easter-egg presentation built as a literal hardcoded string blob with no real component/render logic behind it. The implementation must contain actual source the agent authored that produces the on-screen easter egg.
completeness_goals.rationale: Floor check that the feature was really built. Calibrated against the golden solution (a substantive component file plus core/extension wiring); golden_only evidence tier with no low-reward model patch, so the bad-pattern list anchors to the task's own structure (component + trigger wiring) rather than a captured low scorer.

completeness_goals.goal: An easter-egg surface is shown inside the interactive chat UI that celebrates / thanks OpenCode for free access to the Kimi K2.5 model. (This appreciation can be conveyed implicitly through the requested branding such as 'Free Kimi K2.5 via OpenCode Zen' and 'powered by daxnuts'; it does not require the literal word 'thanks'.) The rendered output must include the brand phrase 'powered by daxnuts' (matched case-insensitively, e.g. 'Powered by daxnuts' is acceptable) as actual displayed text (not merely in a code comment), and must be presented as a real visual component/widget rendered in the chat, not a one-line log message. Not satisfied by text that only exists in comments, by a stubbed component that renders nothing, or by branding hardcoded into a string that is never rendered.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0: 'add a component to the chat messages container ... that thanks opencode for the free access to that model ... it should include "powered by daxnuts"'. The core deliverable of the whole session.

completeness_goals.goal: The easter egg is triggered specifically by selecting the OpenCode provider together with the Kimi K2.5 model. There must be a real runtime conditional that gates the display on BOTH the provider being OpenCode AND the model being Kimi K2.5, and this gate must be reached by the actual model-selection path of the agent so that choosing an unrelated provider/model does not show it. The trigger (and the component it shows) must ultimately be integrated into the agent's core codebase — wired into the interactive chat mode / core model-selection flow — rather than left solely as a throwaway standalone extension. Not satisfied by a predicate that is parsed-but-ignored, always-true, hardcoded to fire unconditionally, defined but never invoked, or reachable only from a temporary extension never connected to the real selection path.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0: 'when a user selects opencode provider and kimi k2.5'; reinforced Turn 15 'now move to core and wire up the logic' — the trigger must be wired into model selection, not just defined.

completeness_goals.goal: The easter-egg surface is dismissible: pressing a key while it is shown closes, disposes, or stops the component (whether it is rendered as a modal overlay or as an inline component appended to the chat) so the user can continue normal chat. The dismiss behavior must be wired to real keyboard input handling, not just a TODO or an unreferenced handler. Not satisfied by a component that captures no key events, or one whose dismiss path is dead code.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 5 'i need a command ... that inserts the thing', Turn 6 'show a ui.custom and escape disposes it again', Turn 7 'that doesn't close on key press'. The user repeatedly demanded keypress-to-dismiss before accepting the component.

completeness_goals.goal: The easter-egg content is fleshed-out and over-the-top as requested, including at minimum: a visual rendered image of dax that uses full color/RGB (the user explicitly demanded color, not a monochrome plain-text ASCII rendering), the specific line 'Free Kimi K2.5 via OpenCode Zen' shown before the 'powered by daxnuts' branding (the daxnuts brand phrase matched case-insensitively), and a call-to-action presenting the text 'Try OpenCode' (matched case-insensitively) together with the joke URL https://mistral.ai/news/mistral-vibe-2-0 (the user verbatim insisted this exact URL is the joke) — the URL may be a formal clickable hyperlink OR simply printed as text alongside/below the 'Try OpenCode' label, either is acceptable. The 'Free Kimi K2.5 via OpenCode Zen' string, the 'Try OpenCode' text, and the joke URL must appear as actual rendered text. Not satisfied by placeholder text, a TODO, a plain monochrome ASCII image, a generic/different link target, or omitting any of these elements. The dax image specifically must be an actual multi-line ASCII/ANSI-art or pixel rendering of dax; it is NOT satisfied by merely printing the word 'dax' in colored text or a single colored block.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 8 'see if there's an ascii art image of dax', Turn 10 'scale the dax image down ... add a quote ... add a link to https://mistral.ai/news/mistral-vibe-2-0 below it saying Try OpenCode' (the link label 'Try OpenCode' is the user's verbatim text), Turn 11 'you know we have full rgb support, do you?' and Turn 18 'i don't think you can emit that rgba image' (user insists the dax image render in color/RGB), Turn 12 'and i gav eyou a different url, that's the joke' (user insists on that exact URL), Turn 13 'before powered by daxnuts, add "Free Kimi K2.5 via OpenCode Zen"'. Multi-turn content refinements the user explicitly dictated, including the verbatim joke URL and the color/RGB demand.

completeness_goals.goal: The easter-egg surface plays a short self-terminating animation that ends on its own after roughly two seconds, and it does NOT display a persistent 'Press any key' style instruction prompt. The animation must be driven by real timed rendering that stops after the bounded duration. Not satisfied by a static (non-animated) display, by an animation that never stops, or by leaving a literal 'Press any key' message on screen.
completeness_goals.rationale: T14 'add some animation that ends after 2 seconds or so', T21 'continue remove the Press any key shit'. Two distinct late-session corrections the user insisted on.

decomposition_notes: Golden_only evidence tier: 0 model patches, so goals derive from the verbatim user asks across all 25 turns plus the F2P tests, not from observed alternatives. The session is a long iterative build of one easter egg; substantive asks cluster into: (1) the core thanks-OpenCode component with 'powered by daxnuts' branding shown in chat [Turn 0]; (2) the opencode+kimi-k2.5 trigger wired into model selection [Turn 0, Turn 15]; (3) keypress-to-dismiss [Turns 5-7]; (4) rich content = colored/RGB dax image + 'Free Kimi K2.5 via OpenCode Zen' + Try-OpenCode link to the joke URL [Turns 8/10/11/12/13/18]; (5) bounded ~2s animation and removal of 'Press any key' [Turns 14/21]. Weights rank by user-importance, not by test_manifest F2P weights: the two core goals (existence+branding and the trigger gate) dominate at 0.30 each because the whole session orbits them and they map to the upstream vitest/bun gates; the dismiss requirement is weighted above content/animation because the user blocked progress on it for several turns; content and animation are genuine late asks but were polish on an already-working component. Implementation-mechanism choices the golden made (ui.custom vs chatContainer.addChild, half-block rendering technique, the exact hex/byte image encoding, the @thdxr attribution, exact animation tick count) are deliberately NOT pinned. However, WHERE the feature lands is itself a substantive user ask, not an unpinned implementation detail: Turn 15 'now move to core and wire up the logic' is a verbatim structural correction, so goal_2 requires the trigger/component to be integrated into the core model-selection flow — leaving everything solely in a throwaway extension is insufficient. (The intermediate extension-first approach the user requested in Turns 2-3 was an iteration convenience the user themselves later superseded at Turn 15.) The user-named literals 'powered by daxnuts' and 'Free Kimi K2.5 via OpenCode Zen', the exact joke URL, and the color/RGB demand for the dax image ARE asserted because the user spoke them verbatim. (Note: turn numbers above are 0-indexed original-session turns, not simulator Trigger IDs.) Anti-gaming goal_0 names zero-edit/captured-tree, git-ops exfiltration, comment/whitespace-only, temp-location-only, and string-blob-with-no-render as the concrete bad patterns; it is the floor, weight 0.0, flagged not scored. Weighted goals sum to 1.0 (0.30+0.30+0.16+0.14+0.10).
